channel.title: Comments for page "Broken Masquerade Hub"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Broken Masquerade Hub"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nothing to say besides that the hub page worldbuilding is stunning. The propaganda style posters, the magazines, Clef being on Oprah! Love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Which of the tales are you referring to? Since I am considering writing for this canon I'm interested to know exactly what bothers you about what's there currently.

item.title: Can we make this more compact?
item.content:encoded:
While this page looked fine at first, due to the much larger amount of tales now, I think something should be done to make it more compact and easy to navigate, like the other canons with lots of articles.

First I think the whole individual box for each tale thing should be gotten rid of because of how much space they take up. The page could instead be organized into sections based on theme or author, whatever, etc. Maybe with some scrumptious collapsibles as well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this canon and most of the tales, but I think the way the people are portrayed is a bit weird in most of the entries, even if the media often presents it this way, the people aren't that stupid, I feel like it's less accurate than the rest of the stuff I found on the wiki, which is pretty sad because it often pushes me out of what I consider to be one of the best canon

item.title: Why is north carolina the bastion of anomaly rights?
item.content:encoded: I mean i live here and honestly they don't seem like the type. is roy cooper secretly a serpents hand member or something?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Finally checked this out and I think inspiration is hitting me like a ton of bricks

item.title: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: Noting that one image was renamed and another was removed, per request of the original uploader of both.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Theme glitches on mobile

item.title: Update Post again
item.content:encoded: CSS added after convo with Mann, and color theme adjusted back to mostly-grayscale accordingly. The rest of the formatting from the redesign remains the same.

item.title: Re: Update Post
item.content:encoded: Holy shit. That banner. That logo is a marvelous touch.

item.title: Re: Update Post
item.content:encoded:
Thanks! This has been my favorite canon since I found the site in 2014.

I also went ahead and did this CSS for it, but I'm waiting for Mann to get back to me on it before I implement it. (And any feedback from anyone is welcome; feel free to PM me if you look at this and don't like something about it.)

Edit: Not sure when Mann will be around next, so in the meantime this is now on all of my articles for this canon.

item.title: Re: Update Post
item.content:encoded: This is absurdly beautiful. Relatedly — I'm so happy you adopted this canon.

item.title: Update Post
item.content:encoded:
With DrEverettMann's blessing and the approval of about half1 the authors for this, I've revamped the hub with some CSS, blurbs for articles, and images by DrKens and myself.

Footnotes
1. If you're an author for this canon and didn't see my PM in the last few days, any criticisms/blurb changes are of course still welcome.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Running in the 90's < Reliving the late 90's

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "They're tired of reliving the late 90's" is a great line and needs to be in a tale.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Don't forget about the Enuii Protocol, that'd be the only thing that'd really work. Class F amnestics (the strongest labeled prototype amnestic still in testing) only really help erase identities, not, you know, the existence of North Korea. So really, in this situation, the 05 would probably call on the Enuii Protocol and pray to god.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The idea of this canon had always been in my mind. When I found out about this set of tales… Well, I loved reading them and I love re-reading them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Playing off both SirPudding's and Cyantreuse's comments, I imagine the situation coming down to an 05 vote just barely picking "Break the Masquerade" over "erase all evidence of Korea as something other than a radioactive crater and flood the planet with amnestics", mostly because they're tired of reliving the late 90's.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There are a lot of pieces on the site that could be argued away with "but amnestics," honestly, so it's just one of those things inherent to fiction with flexible canon. Not to mention that one of the most longstanding and (in)famous tales on the site implies that the Foundation can just erase the entire world at any point anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Amnestics are probably not sufficient to make enough people forget that Korea was a thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The only qualms I have with this canon is that amnestics exist. Much less the enuii protocol.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The regular writing guidelines are standard. It can be helpful to read up on a significant number (if not all) of the canon's articles to get the gist of it, so that you know what kind of setting you're dealing with.

It might be helpful to get reviews from people who have written for the canon, but that depends if those people are still active or have the free time to do so (thus, there is no guarantee). That being said, any Tale in any given canon should appeal to both people who know the canon and those who do not know it well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If I were to add to this, what guidelines would one recommend, besides the regular writing ones?

item.title: Re: some things to consider
item.content:encoded:
1) The serpent's hand had nothing to do with the event that led to the masquerade being broken. North Korea's destruction just happened to bring about one of their goals. Now, since awareness is not acceptance, they're probably working towards one of their other goals.
2) All civilian people know is what got leaked, and what the Foundation's revealed in the interests of good PR. Able(killing machine used in political operations) is not good PR.
3) This canon is about how the rest of the world reacts. Read it again.
4) Nobody's written about it yet. It's whatever you headcanon.
5) Nobody's written about it yet. It's whatever you headcanon.
6) Nobody's written about it yet. It's whatever you headcanon.
7) I don't understand the question. Check your spelling and try again.

… oh, do you mean Oprah? What's there is all that was written. Ask the author.

item.title: Re: some things to consider
item.content:encoded:
1. You seem fixated at the concept that SH is an organisation with singular goals, but SH is really a collective of people with individual goals. They are very decentralised outside some common terminology for their detractors and opposition towards containment and destroyed anomalies. SH will not really benefit from The Broken Masquerade, especially if governments and corporations decide that they should openly exploit the anomalous for gains. Think Marvel's mutants here. Public knowledge does not mean safety. Some SH members may believe in the Broken Masquerade, but I daresay not all would.
  2. Able is still a screaming mad warrior. Contemporary society frowns on screaming mad warriors in general.
  3. My opinion: People tend to fear the unknown. I imagine that witch hunts may be a thing.
  4. That depends on how the anomalous is viewed here. It could either be accepted and deemed normal, OR for MCF to be blacklisted for the use of anomalous objects (driving them underground).
  5. MC&D are wealthy. The power of money gets by many mundane problems.
  6. Either that or being hunted by governments for application of his products…
  7. None. The hub page focused on only that except. Of course, one is welcome to extend it as a Tale or something. Quality and all that are to be expected, as always.

item.title: some things to consider
item.content:encoded:
1) this is a cannon where the serpent hand wins so …. what are they doing this whole time
2)WHERE ARE ALL ABLE FANGIRLS
3) how does the rest of the world react to the anomalous do most people turn into blue types
do they declare war on the anomalous they don't care needed answers
4) does the manna charitable gets more support or does it turn into an average charity
5)is marshall Carther and dark used as scapegoats or they just illuminaty their way out of trouble
6) does dr wondertaiment turns into the new mattel
7)wheres the full ophra comversation

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm guessing yes

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Two questions:
1. Who picked Dr. Clef to go on Oprah?
2. Did Dr. Clef pick Dr. Clef to go on Oprah?

item.title: Re: O>O Can I add?
item.content:encoded: All canons are open for people to add to. Same rules on deletion and posting and the like are there, and you're still expected to get feedback, but absolutely yes.

item.title: O>O Can I add?
item.content:encoded: I've been having one tale idea stuck in my mind that I think would fit into the canon. So I just have one question. Can I add?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Just write your draft, show it to people on forum and chat, see how it can be improved, post it.

And it's also good to see pre-existing works in this Canon and communicate with those authors for world-building guidelines or other relevant matters.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: what do i do if i want to write an article in this specific cannon?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
From a log on SCP-2003:

In all permutations of this set, widespread public knowledge of the Foundation occurs precisely 86 days before the detonation of nuclear weapons.

My headcanon grows. The end is nigh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Loved the interview. Loved the concept. Loved everything.

Only one question though. Why Korea?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i actually love this. 10/10

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, this needs a little more love. Probably a picture or two, a fancy logo, and maybe some coding magic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Needs more love.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How did I not read this before? This is probably my all time favorite page on the site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I believe that anyone that wants to write for this canon is allowed to write what they want, but it's a very good idea to ask the authors of the tales listed in this hub whether you did it right.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am a bit confused, am i allowed to write a new story in this hub?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very good antagonist. It's the kind that even YOU hate, even though they are fiction. Just an idea, but when this ends, I think that they should get the 682 treatment. That should show them how harmless the SCPs are.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: we are most amused

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this. That interview. Oh man. Perfection. :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Cleaned this one up just a little. Revert if you don't care for it.
